explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: A privacy policy document implies the need for oversight of vendors involved in tracking and personalization activities.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This privacy policy document provides evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars. It supports the privacy pillar by describing the collection and use of personal data for purposes such as advertising optimization, web analysis, and service matching, which involve automated systems and profiling. The document also supports the governance pillar by implying the need for oversight of vendors like Google involved in these tracking and personalization activities.
relevant_sources.title: BrightSpring Health Services Privacy Policy

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: A privacy policy document describes the collection and use of personal data for purposes such as advertising optimization, web analysis, and service matching. This document states that these activities involve automated systems and profiling.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

relevant_sources.summary: This privacy policy document provides evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars. It supports the privacy pillar by describing the collection and use of personal data for purposes such as advertising optimization, web analysis, and service matching, which involve automated systems and profiling. The document also supports the governance pillar by implying the need for oversight of vendors like Google involved in these tracking and personalization activities.
relevant_sources.title: BrightSpring Health Services Privacy Policy

transparency.findings: The Louisville AI Week Agenda references AI use cases. A third-party case study describes the AI-driven capabilities of the Hyro conversational AI voice assistant, detailing its impact on call resolution and self-service rates.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

relevant_sources.summary: The Louisville AI Week Agenda, a third-party document, provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar of responsible AI. It mentions "AI use cases," indicating an awareness of and potential for transparent communication about AI applications, although the excerpt is aspirational and lacks specific operational or policy details.
relevant_sources.title: Louisville AI Week Agenda

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party case study, "BrightSpring Health Services Leverages AI for IT Support," provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar. The case study describes the AI-driven capabilities of the Hyro conversational AI voice assistant, detailing its impact on call resolution and self-service rates. While it highlights the benefits and functionalities of the AI solution for IT support, it does not include specific operational or governance mechanisms.
relevant_sources.title: BrightSpring Health Services Leverages AI for IT Support

summary.overall_findings: Based on 7 publicly available sources, BrightSpring Health Services' published materials describe privacy policies that detail the collection and use of personal data for advertising optimization and web analysis. These disclosures address 2 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars, including governance, where a policy document implies the need for oversight of vendors involved in tracking and personalization activities. No qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
